events.text: Patient is a 73-year-old male of Black/African American ethnicity, non-Hispanic, BMI 29.5, with a history of hypertension, chronic kidney disease stage 3 (baseline creatinine 1.6), and benign prostatic hyperplasia.
At age 72, patient presented with abdominal distension and was found to have a large bowel obstruction; emergent CT revealed an obstructing cecal mass (C18.0) with liver metastases.
At age 72, patient underwent emergent right hemicolectomy with primary anastomosis for obstructing cecal tumor.

Right hemicolectomy specimen showed poorly differentiated adenocarcinoma (histology 8140/3) of the cecum with transmural invasion into pericolic fat and serosal involvement, pT4aN2b (8 of 24 lymph nodes positive), lymphovascular and perineural invasion present.

Tumor genomic profiling revealed KRAS G12D mutation, BRAF wild-type, PIK3CA E542K co-mutation, SMAD4 deletion, TP53 missense mutation; microsatellite stable (MSS) with proficient mismatch repair; PD-L1 CPS less than 1.

Postoperative CT chest/abdomen/pelvis at age 72 confirmed multiple bilobar hepatic metastases (6 lesions, largest 4.5 cm in segment VIII) and two indeterminate pulmonary nodules (6 mm and 8 mm in right lung); staged pT4aN2bM1b.
At age 72, baseline postoperative CEA was 167 ng/mL and CA 19-9 was 203 U/mL; right-sided primary with high-volume metastatic disease; palliative systemic therapy indicated.

At age 72, given chronic kidney disease, nephrology consultation obtained; recommended hydration protocol with chemotherapy and avoidance of nephrotoxic agents; creatinine clearance estimated at 42 mL/min.
At age 72, patient started first-line FOLFIRI (with irinotecan dose-adjusted for renal function) plus bevacizumab every 2 weeks.
After cycle 3 of FOLFIRI-bevacizumab, patient developed grade 2 diarrhea and grade 1 nausea; irinotecan reduced by 20% with improvement.

At age 72, patient developed grade 3 hypertension (BP 184/102) attributed to bevacizumab in the setting of pre-existing hypertension and CKD; required addition of amlodipine to existing antihypertensive regimen.
Restaging CT after 6 cycles showed stable to mixed response in hepatic metastases; pulmonary nodules stable; CEA decreased to 89 ng/mL.
At age 73, CA 19-9 decreased to 124 U/mL; creatinine stable at 1.7; patient tolerating adjusted FOLFIRI-bevacizumab with ECOG 1.
At age 73, patient experienced grade 2 fatigue limiting activities of daily living; discussed with patient and family, decided to continue treatment with supportive measures.
CT at age 73 after 12 cycles demonstrated progressive disease with new hepatic lesions and enlarging pulmonary nodules now measuring 12 mm and 14 mm; CEA rose to 198 ng/mL.
At age 73, transitioned to second-line TAS-102 (trifluridine/tipiracil) given concerns about regorafenib tolerability in the setting of CKD and hypertension.
At age 73, developed grade 3 neutropenia on TAS-102 requiring dose reduction and G-CSF support with pegfilgrastim.
CT after 2 cycles of TAS-102 showed continued progression; CEA 287 ng/mL; ECOG performance status declined to 2.
At age 73, goals of care meeting held with patient and family; elected to transition to comfort-focused care with hospice referral given progressive disease refractory to multiple lines of therapy.
At age 73, final CEA was 287 ng/mL and CA 19-9 was 341 U/mL; patient enrolled in home hospice with comfort-focused symptom management.

scenario_blurb: Stage IV colorectal cancer with KRAS G12D mutation. Left-sided and right-sided primary tumors. Treated with FOLFOX or FOLFIRI plus bevacizumab. Some with liver-limited metastases considered for hepatic resection. MSI/MMR testing performed.
